title: Migrate Rick Terminal SwiftUI views to @Observable + Lazy stacks
source: Apple SwiftUI guidance: Observation framework (@Observable replacing ObservableObject), LazyVStack/Table virtualization, ScrollViewReader auto-scroll (knowledge through Jan 2026)
description: If the SwiftUI Rick Terminal still uses ObservableObject/@Published it re-renders broadly on every roro event, which degrades as the event log grows during long sprints. Migrating to the @Observable macro narrows invalidation to the properties actually read by each view, and rendering the event/ticket stream in a LazyVStack or Table with stable Identifiable ids plus ScrollViewReader auto-scroll keeps the UI smooth with thousands of events.
proposed_changes: In the SwiftUI app (path TBD — locate the .xcodeproj/Sources dir; placeholder shown), replace ObservableObject view models with the @Observable macro and swap @ObservedObject/@StateObject for @State/@Bindable. Render the roro event feed in a LazyVStack or Table backed by an array of Identifiable events with stable ids. Add a ScrollViewReader that pins to the latest event unless the user has scrolled up. Verify minimum deployment target supports the Observation framework before migrating.
reject_reason: No files were actually modified (false success)
